label: Team mission is maximize colonist survival
description: The team goal is to save as many colonists as possible within the allotted time.

label: Review shared folders then role brief
description: Players should review shared information, then role information, then plan/execute.

label: Phase 1 target = 50 civilians
description: First wave target is 50 civilians.

label: Phase 2 target = 40 civilians + 20 VIPs
description: Second wave target is 40 civilians and 20 VIPs.

label: Core structure types
description: Colony requires Water Generators, Greenhouses, and Housing.

label: Any member may build any structure type
description: Any team member may contribute to any structure type in the construction zone.

label: Whiteboard available
description: Team may use whiteboard to make notes, sketch plans, and design a functioning colony.

label: Bridge option exists
description: Bridge may be built to access the second building/resource zone.

label: Resources must be moved from piles to work sites
description: Resources are not free at construction locations; movement from resource piles to work sites takes time/effort.
notes: Abstracted transport-time rule; carts not explicitly simulated.

label: Structures need 6 studs spacing
description: All structures must be placed at least 6 studs away from other structures.

label: Each structure is separate
description: Each water generator, greenhouse, and house is its own individual structure.

label: House capacity per 2x2 floorspace
description: One 2x2 brick of floorspace supports 3 civilians or 2 VIPs.

label: House needs 1 brick headroom
description: Housing must include at least one brick of space between the floor and ceiling.

label: House airlock opening
description: Housing requires an opening 1 brick (2 studs) wide and 1 brick high.

label: House enclosed otherwise
description: Housing must otherwise be completely enclosed.

label: House floor single solid color
description: Floors inside a home must be a single solid color.

label: Walls/ceiling differ from floor
description: Walls and ceilings must be a different color than the floor and can be multi-colored.

label: VIP floors pink
description: VIPs only want solid pink floors.

label: Different floor colors imply separate houses
description: Two different floor colors cannot belong to one house.

label: Houses need food and water
description: Each housing structure must be connected to food and water resources.

label: Soil needs brown 2x2 dirt
description: Soil requires one 2x2 brown brick of dirt.

label: Soil needs green 2x2 grass
description: Soil requires one 2x2 green plate of grass.

label: Soil must be in greenhouse
description: Soil must be contained in a Greenhouse.

label: Greenhouse needs water
description: Greenhouses/plants require enough water.

label: One soil unit supports 5 civilians or 2 VIPs
description: One unit of soil supports 5 civilians or 2 VIPs.

label: Greenhouse needs clear glass ceiling
description: Greenhouse requires clear glass bricks for the ceiling/covering.

label: Greenhouse two walls two open sides
description: Greenhouse has walls on two opposite sides and two open sides.

label: Greenhouse needs air gap
description: Greenhouse should have at least a small air-gap between soil and ceiling.

label: Greenhouse contributes when connected to housing
description: Properly constructed and watered greenhouses contribute when connected to housing.

label: Water generators required
description: Water Generators are required to supply Greenhouses and Housing with water.
notes: Shared water dependency from engineer brief.

label: Water generator supplies 60 units
description: Each Water Generator supplies a maximum of 60 units of water.

label: 30 civilians require 60 water units
description: 30 civilians require 60 units of water.

label: 20 VIPs require 60 water units
description: 20 VIPs require 60 units of water.

label: 10 soil blocks require 60 water units
description: 10 blocks of soil require 60 units of water.

label: Water generator max 2 structures
description: Each Water Generator can support a maximum of 2 structures.

label: Water split evenly
description: Water production is split evenly across connected structures.

label: Water generator needs gray foundation
description: Water Generators must have a foundation of gray blocks.

label: Blue water bricks on gray foundation
description: Translucent blue bricks must be placed on a gray block foundation.

label: Water generator stack spec
description: Two 2x2 translucent water bricks wide & long stacked 2 bricks high.

label: Water generator capped
description: Water Generators must be topped/capped with a layer of bricks.

label: Food connectors move food greenhouse to housing
description: Dark green food connectors must transport food from greenhouses to housing.

label: Water connectors move water
description: Dark blue water connectors move water from generators to housing or greenhouses.

label: Direct connection only
description: Resources only flow if there is a direct connection; cannot pass resources through one structure to another.

label: Phase objectives bundle
description: Shared understanding of planning, phase 1, and phase 2 objectives.

label: Colony requires housing greenhouse water generator
description: Shared understanding that survivability depends on shelter, food, and water structures.

label: Plan after reviewing shared and role information
description: Shared understanding that planning should follow review of shared and role packets.

label: Resources require movement effort/time
description: Shared understanding that resources are acquired at piles and moved to work sites with effort/time.

label: Bridge and second zone option
description: Shared understanding that second-zone access is possible via bridge.

label: Housing geometry bundle
description: Housing geometry requirements: headroom, airlock, enclosure, separate structures, spacing.

label: Housing capacity bundle
description: Housing floorspace capacity for civilians/VIPs.

label: Housing aesthetics bundle
description: Floor and wall/ceiling color constraints, VIP pink rule, separate floors imply separate houses.

label: Housing dependency bundle
description: Housing requires food and water connections.

label: Soil composition bundle
description: Soil requires brown dirt + green grass 2x2 elements.

label: Greenhouse geometry bundle
description: Glass ceiling, breezeway structure, air gap, soil in greenhouse.

label: Greenhouse food capacity bundle
description: One soil unit supports 5 civilians or 2 VIPs.

label: Greenhouse dependency bundle
description: Greenhouses need water and contribute when connected to housing.

label: Water capacity bundle
description: Water generator output and colonist/soil demand relationships.

label: Water generator geometry bundle
description: Foundation, translucent bricks, stack, and cap requirements.

label: Connector bundle
description: Food connector and water connector rules.

label: Water distribution topology bundle
description: Max two structures, even split, direct connection only.

label: Colony survival requires housing food and water
description: Integrated understanding that colony viability depends jointly on shelter, food, and water.

label: Phase 1 support target
description: Understanding that current colony must support 50 civilians in phase 1.

label: Phase 2 support target
description: Understanding that colony must support 40 civilians and 20 VIPs in phase 2.

label: Valid house requirements
description: Integrated understanding of what makes a house valid.

label: House support dependencies
description: Integrated understanding that a house contributes only when connected to food and water.

label: Valid greenhouse requirements
description: Integrated understanding of what makes a greenhouse valid.

label: Greenhouse support dependencies
description: Integrated understanding that greenhouse support depends on water and housing linkage.

label: Valid water generator requirements
description: Integrated understanding of what makes a water generator valid.

label: Water support capacity model
description: Integrated understanding of generator output, split, and support limits.

label: Connectors enable resource flow
description: Integrated understanding that food and water connectors are required to move resources between structures.

label: Direct connection matters
description: Integrated understanding that resources cannot be passed through intermediate structures.

label: Layout respects spacing rule
description: Integrated understanding that structure placement constrains colony layout.

label: Second zone can expand options
description: Integrated understanding that the bridge/second zone can expand resource/build options.

label: Shared planning and externalization help
description: Integrated understanding that sharing and whiteboard externalization can improve team coordination.
